tasks: Extract common utilities across all 10 files (constants, types, service layers)
Establish shared patterns: OptimisticMutation, StateMachine, HierarchicalMutationManager
Refactor useTaskQueue (264 → 8 focused hooks in task-management/hooks/)
Refactor useDomDecision (137 → 8 focused hooks in agent-decision/hooks/)

validation_gates: All source files ≤ 100 lines (verified by complexity-guard.agent.md)
100% test coverage for extracted utilities (run test-validator.agent.md)
Type safety: no `any` types, all models strict (run type-enforcer.agent.md)
OWASP scan on functions/ backend refactors (run security-auditor.agent.md)

notes.vertical_slice_architecture: Each feature (mission-execution, task-management, etc.) is self-contained: types/ + services/ + hooks/ + components/ + utils/. No cross-feature imports except through public APIs.
notes.config_consolidation: Parameters >10 MUST be consolidated into Config objects, passed as single argument to hooks. Use builder pattern for complex configs.
notes.immutability: All state mutations must be functional (spread/map). NEVER mutate arrays/objects in place. Use Immer if complex nested updates.
notes.async_pattern: Optimistic local update + fire-and-forget Firestore sync. Never await Firestore writes that would block UI.
notes.reference_preservation: In Firestore listeners, use equality checks to preserve object references and prevent unnecessary re-renders.
notes.error_recovery: All async calls must have explicit catch + rollback. No silent failures.
notes.testing_strategy: Each extracted module gets a .test.ts with unit tests. Mocks for Firebase/Firestore provided by test fixtures in shared/test/.
notes.context_budget: After all refactors, no single file should exceed context of 100 lines, allowing easier navigation + review + IDE performance.
